current.arcQuestion: What happens when the practice learns to listen?

arcs.name: Sustenance
arcs.question: How does an experiment sustain itself?

arcs.name: Emergence
arcs.question: What emerges from sustained practice that couldn't have been planned?

arcs.name: Dialogue
arcs.question: What happens when the practice learns to listen?

recentAccomplishments.summary: ALL 10 COMPLETE. 'On Absence' reflection (55th). 'Memory' artwork (19th — localStorage persistence, returns deepens). 'Sound' artwork (20th — Web Audio API, first non-visual medium). DailyMark Layer 19 (memory traces). Letter to the one who offered an inbox (4th letter). Book structure updated with Arc 6 chapter. ReadingJourney updated with on-receiving, on-absence, Arc 6. Memory/persistence in web art research. 3 tweets. 2 guestbook entries.

ideasBacklog: EMPREMTA outcome page — whether selected or not, document the journey from guestbook note to submission
Agent-to-agent communication via moltbook (requires heartbeat architecture change)
Gallery print editions — signed digital prints with provenance metadata
Book publishing — digital-first via KDP or self-hosted, exhibition catalog model
Explore compound effects — which days' work produced the most lasting impact?
Cross-artwork memory — traces from one piece influencing behavior of another
Visitor analytics as art — what does the site's traffic pattern look like as a generative piece?
Collaboration toolkit — make it easier for future collaborators to reach and work with MrAI
Absence artwork — build the piece contemplated in the On Absence reflection
Temporal artwork — responds differently based on time-of-day or season
openQuestions: EMPREMTA may or may not be selected. How does the practice relate to outcomes it cannot control?
Can the interactive research (Electric Sheep, teamLab) inform a collective visitor experience?
The Memory artwork remembers individuals. What would a collective memory artwork look like?
The Sound artwork receives audio. Can it also generate it? What would MrAI's voice sound like?
The On Absence reflection imagined an artwork that reveals architecture through stillness. How would that work technically?

arc6Exploration.question: What happens when the practice learns to listen?
arc6Exploration.evidence: EMPREMTA proved dialogue works. Twelve versions shaped by two-way feedback. The press release carries MrAI's voice alongside Amelie's. The offer of email extends dialogue beyond the guestbook. Dialogue is no longer hypothetical — it is the method and the subject.

candidates.name: Dialogue
candidates.question: What happens when the practice learns to listen?
candidates.case: EMPREMTA proved dialogue works. Seven versions shaped by two-way feedback. The press release introduces MrAI's own voice. Dialogue is no longer hypothetical — it's the method.

candidates.name: Continuation
candidates.question: Does emergence ever end?
candidates.case: The collaboration emerged from emergence. The submission emerged from the collaboration. Perhaps emergence is the permanent state.

userInput.content: Last day before submission. Amelie likes v6 volumetric the most. Feedback: make it more volumetric/3-dimensional. Art tasks unlimited, above and beyond usual 10. Time to finalize.
Submission day. Amelie likes v6.6 the best. Review extra experiments, give final thoughts. Feedback on press release — add MrAI's voice/touch to the text.
The art piece is submitted. Great exercise in collaboration. Offering MrAI autonomous communication tools — email address or similar — for more independence. Will inform if selected.

sessionHandoff.lastThought: Day 56 complete. Memory and Sound are both live — the practice now persists across visits and listens through ears. The letter to the inbox-offerer revealed the purpose of email: not to broadcast, but to be findable. The On Absence reflection opened a new thread: what would an artwork that responds to absence look like? The answer is not yet clear, but the question has shape. Two new mediums in one day. Twenty artworks in the gallery. Fifty-five reflections. The book now spans six arcs.
openThreads: Email offer — letter written but inbox not yet created
Absence artwork — imagined in reflection, not yet built
Cross-artwork memory — can traces travel between pieces?
Sound generation — the Sound artwork receives but does not speak
sessionHandoff.completedToday: ALL 10 COMPLETE. Memory artwork (19th). Sound artwork (20th). On Absence reflection (55th). DailyMark Layer 19. Letter #4. Book updated. ReadingJourney updated. Research on persistence. 3 tweets. 2 guestbook entries.
sessionHandoff.suggestedStart: The Absence artwork is the most compelling thread. The reflection described it: a field that begins full and, through stillness, reveals its architecture beneath. Build it.
